prompt: Close-up, professional product photography of an extraordinarily over-fitted suit jacket on a tailoring dummy in an upscale atelier. The jacket is covered with an excessive number of hand-stitched darts, tucks, and custom seams precisely following every curve and contour. Each seam and dart is visible, intricate, and clearly obsessive. The stitching is perfect but the overall shape is impossible—custom seams curve in ways that no real-world body could match. A measuring tape is draped nearby, along with tailor's chalk marks and pins. The dummy wearing the jacket looks satisfied; beside it on a wooden bench, a simple, well-made traditional suit jacket (not over-fitted) sits peacefully. The over-fitted jacket is clearly unwearable by anyone else. Warm atelier lighting, soft north-facing studio light on cream walls. Shot from a three-quarter angle. No text, no labels, no annotations, no diagrams. Photo-realistic, editorial quality.
metaphor: An extraordinarily over-fitted suit on a tailor's dummy, covered in excessive hand-stitched darts and seams tracking every contour. Beautiful but unwearable—too specific to one dummy, useless for anyone else.
